audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in add.c are guarded: `ber` is null-checked after allocation (L101), `attrs` is guarded by `if (attrs)` (L116) and loop sentinel `attrs[i] != NULL` (L118), `mod_bvalues`/`mod_values`/`bv_val` are explicitly null-checked before use. The only unguarded field, `attrs[i]->mod_type`, is set from `a->a_desc->ad_cname.bv_val` in the primary server-side caller (`back-asyncmeta/add.c:110`), where slapd schema validation ensures a non-NULL canonical attribute name before this path is reached. No concrete path from an external LDAP Add request delivers a NULL `mod_type` to `ber_printf`.
